topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I clicked it open, and the headline screamed my name, but the byline belonged to a complete stranger. A slow, burning heat rose up my neck, making my skin prickle. I reread the text, every word a sharp shard against my chest. How could this be? I sat back in my worn office chair, the springs groaning in protest under my sudden stillness. My hands, which had been poised to type, trembled slightly as I traced the unfamiliar name. A low, ragged sound escaped my throat—a mixture of disbelief and a simmering, deep-seated anger. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small confines of my workspace, the sound of my own footsteps echoing hollowly.

[story 2]

When the notification popped up, I felt the blood drain from my face. It wasn't a shock, exactly, but a sudden, profound sense of being utterly disregarded. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat. The words confirmed the worst fear: my contribution, my carefully crafted narrative, had been lifted and rebranded. A slow, deliberate clenching of my jaw was the only outward sign of the turmoil churning inside me. I walked over to the window, pulling the blinds shut against the glare of the afternoon sun. My knuckles were white where I gripped the sill. A bitter taste flooded my mouth, metallic and sharp. I turned back to the computer, my movements stiff, every gesture radiating a controlled, simmering resentment.

[story 3]

I reread the attribution three times, the words blurring slightly at the edges. A strange, hollow feeling settled over me, heavy and suffocating. It wasn't just that my work was there; it was the deliberate erasure of my authorship. A sharp, involuntary sigh escaped my lips, sounding more like a hiss than a breath. I dropped the mouse onto the desk with a soft thud that seemed deafening in the quiet room. My shoulders tensed, pulling upward towards my ears, a physical manifestation of the tightening coil in my chest. I began to pace toward the door, my steps quick and uneven, a restless energy vibrating off me. When I finally stopped, I stared at the empty space where my original submission should have been, a tight, frustrated grimace pulling at the corners of my mouth.
